Tip number one, this is extremely important for you. You have to master, not dabble, but master in nonverbal communication, all right? So it's not the words you're asking, actually saying, it's how you're saying those words.
Because have you ever wondered why salespeople, maybe in your industry, that sell the same thing to the same prospects to the same price point using the same scripts, why is there such a radical difference in how much they're actually selling? They're doing the same thing, selling the same thing. That's because of their tonality. It's how you're saying those words. It's how you're actually asking the questions. Your tonality, I always... say, is how the prospect interprets your intention behind everything you say and ask. Your facial expressions, believe it or not, okay? Your facial expressions are the remote control to your tone. Okay. Watch, watch your favorite Hollywood actress or actor. Like, if we're talking like this the entire time, like a flat tone and their facial expressions never moved, they would never trigger any of your emotions.
Your facial expressions can quite literally transfer emotions from you to the prospect you're talking to. You. I can teach you how to do that. And then your body language, because your body language affects your tone. Okay. So, if I just sit here straight, it's really hard for my tone to shift up and down. But if I'm moving around, I can actually pace out what I'm actually saying. simply by moving my hands, okay? Now, there's a lot more to that. These things master nonverbal communication. I can assure you, you do that, you're gonna make a lot more sense. sales, this is critical that you understand this, okay? It's not how long you've been in sales. A lot of salespeople have this myth that it's like, if I just stay in long enough, if I get enough reps in, eventually I'll figure it out.
You can play the long game and fail over and over, but if you want to be in the top 1%, you want to succeed right now. And that's all going to be based on your skill level that you start mastering. And because of that, he got up to this level. Has nothing to do with how long. Has everything to do with how much skill you master. That determines how many sales you're actually going to make and what your income is actually going to be. This is a really critical one. Now, the third one, this is obviously really important is here. They learn from the best. Let me repeat that. They learn from the best, okay? Here's what I always said. Your destiny will be determined by those who you learn from. So, if you learn from people who are not that great at sales, your destiny is determined on them and their lack of skill level.
Could be trouble. It'd be like Michael Jordan learning from somebody that wasn't really that good at basketball. It wouldn't make any sense. He wouldn't be Michael Jordan, okay? So you have to find the person or company or whatever to actually learn from that's already doing the thing that you want to do, that's already crushing it in sales. My second year in sales, I hired an acting coach. Pretty good acting coach. You can look him up. He actually is the acting coach for Leonardo DiCaprio. I don't know if I'm pronouncing his... Sorry, Leo, I might not be pronouncing your name right. But this acting coach, I had to pay... him $127,000. I can assure you, I didn't have that money as a 23-year-old at that point.
I had to go beg, borrow, steal. Like I had to. Loan against a house I had just bought. I had to put it on, like, I don't even know how many credit cards, but I went all in because I knew my destiny would be determined by learning from the best. And this guy right here, that acting coach taught me: advanced tonality, facial expressions, body language, everything. And my sales actually about 4X'd the next year. I wasn't bad the first year, but they 4X'd the next year and they kept multiplying every single year. So you want to work and hire the best. That always pays off, I can assure you. Okay, number four. You really have to find the chip on your shoulder. Have you ever watched like the documentary with Tom Brady?
Remember how he was drafted last out of Michigan, sixth round? All the teams didn't want him. And that put that chip on his shoulder where he was so driven, where he wanted to prove every single team wrong that didn't draft him. And that's why he spent all those. Those extra hours every single day, practicing, going through game film, that chip drove him to become the greatest quarterback ever at this time. So what drives you? What's personally driving you? What personally drove me? I'll just tell you. I'll be very real with you. When I was an 11-year-old kid, my parents were middle-class. My stepdad lost his job. He became disabled, okay? We went from a middle-class family to complete like poverty, went on food stamps.
My mom had to go back to work as like a waitress back to school. There was five of us boys, and I remember going to practice that next summer because I was pretty good at baseball at that point and I had those Walmart shoes on. They're like called Voight. They're like cheap shoes. My friends all had the Nikes and the Reeboks and the Adidas shoes on. And I just remember feeling like. so broke and poor. And I'm just like, I'm never going to be poor like that again. Now that sounds kind of crazy, but that freaking drove me to where I was like, I am never going to be broke or poor ever again. It doesn't mean that you have to be broke as a kid.
Maybe you were extremely wealthy and everybody thought you were spoon-fed everything by your parents. And that might drive you to show everybody that they're wrong, that you really can go out on your own and make money. So it doesn't matter. There's always a chip. That drives anybody, you just have to activate that chip. Ah, number five, this is a big one. Risk tolerance, okay? Top salespeople, they're always risk tolerant. Now, here's what I mean by that, okay? Remember when I told you about the acting coach that I went and hired, had to pay 127 grand? I was 23 years old. I think it could really help me, you know, crush in sales. I think I can do two, three, five times more income, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah. He's like, 'Well, why are you coming to me then? You already have the answer.' And he said, 'Jeremy, God feeds the birds.' but he doesn't drop worms in their nest.
God feeds the birds. But he doesn't drop worms. You got to do something like he gives you opportunity.' He puts people in your path that are there to help you. But a lot of us do what? We get scared. We have. Fear and we know we should do something, right? Have you ever been to like an event and you saw like an idea or like maybe a training program you could buy that you knew if you did, it was going to completely change your results, completely change your life forever, yet you still didn't do it. Well, that's a pattern, okay? And we can help you break those patterns if you wanna get into the top 1%. So my bishop helped me break my pattern that was holding me back.
From where I really could be. And I always ask myself this question now with any decision, which is more risky? Is it more risky for me to do nothing, keep selling the same way I always have, make the same amount of sales and the same income over and over again? Or is it more risky for me to actually go all in, get the funding, hire somebody, train them, make more sales, and my income changes forever? I mean, in reality. Which is more risky, right? So that's how I always frame, like if I'm ever looking at a decision, which is more risky: do nothing, problems stay the same, or get funding, problems get solved, and I get what I want, okay? So ask yourself. Which is more risky, okay?
This is an important one, especially this one coming up. Make sure you listen and write this down, okay? This is commitment to mastery. I want you to think about who your favorite person is. Maybe a doctor you follow, or maybe an influencer you follow, or an athlete, or a musician, or an artist you follow. Were they committed to mastery? More than likely, yes. I would say there's four types of salespeople. And I know, listen, I'm not saying you're that type of person. I'm gonna show you those though. There's the wingers, okay? Those salespeople wing everything, right? They just say different things to every different type of prospect and they just kind of hope and pray that something they're gonna say is gonna magically cause that prospect to wanna buy.
And they don't know why somebody buys, why they don't buy. They just wing it every day, okay? These salespeople, they always blame the leads. They blame the prospects. Oh, the prospects are broke. They've got too much fear. Well, yes, it's your job to help them overcome that fear. That's why you're in sales, right? And like I said, they always hope and pray everything's going to work out. And where do those reps go? Most of these reps get... fired. And I know that's not you for sure. Now, the second one, these I call the dabblers. They kind of dabble in sales, right? They buy a few books every day. Year, 20, 30 bucks. They go to a couple of free events or cheap events. They watch, watch some basic, you know, free content on IG or YouTube.
And they think, 'Hey, if I stay in long enough, if I stay in for years, eventually I'll figure it out.' Now, these are the reps that play, unfortunately, because they lack the skill level, because they're dabbling, they have to play, they're forced to play the numbers game. These salespeople are typically average if they work. Really, really hard because they play the numbers game. They put in, you know, 10 plus 12 hours a day. Maybe eventually they get good after years of losing deals. They could be, these are the dabblers. I love these people. These are the know-it-allers. You know who they are, salespeople, right? The know-it-allers. They have a huge ego. They think they know everything.
Is to know about selling once they get up to like 100 grand a year, 150, 250, 400, 500 grand a year, and the know-it-allers, they get capped. Because they stop acquiring skills. They think they know everything, okay? And they get stopped. They stop training. And when they stop training, income stops as well. I mean, think about it logically, like Steph Curry, basketball, okay, Hall of Famer. What would happen if he stopped training every day? The know-it-allers, they always get. You never want to be a know-it-aller. These people never get to where they could be if they kept training. These are the ones, these are committed to mastery. This is where everybody wants to be. These salespeople, they invest funding. Even if they don't have it, they go find it from somewhere.
It's important that they invest funding because they know the number one thing tied in. to what they're going to earn is their what? Their skill level. So they're investing funds and funding all the time to acquire more skill levels. They're always selling more, okay? Number two, because they do that, they don't make the same mistakes that other salespeople make. The most successful people in life do not learn from their mistakes. They learn from other people's mistakes. So they don't make the same mistakes themselves. Okay. So less mistakes than sales means you make more sales. All right. They always train daily. So they're training daily. They know they're getting better every single day. And these that are committed to mastery, they overcome the learning curve 100x faster. They're all about, I don't want to fail.
I spend months or years trying to figure it on my own and fail when I can just learn from the best and I can succeed right now. So they're all about speed to succeed. Because of that, they get into the top 1% or actually the top 0. 01%. When I was a salesperson, I learned this from the people I was around in my first company. worked for. I never hung out with the wingers, the dabblers, because they're always jealous that you're outselling them. I definitely did not want to hang out with the know-it-allers. I wanted to be around the ones who were committed to mastery, because we were always talking about sales, persuasion, influence. Oh, did you know this? Could you learn this? This course you should get.
This, because it's going to teach you this. And I'd always get that information from them. And we would trade that information. And that helped us get into that 0. 01%. Okay. What's the saying? You are who you associate yourself with. The top five people you typically hang out with is where your income's going to average. So you want to get around these people. They don't wake up on Monday at seven, you know, and Tuesday at 5:30 a.m. and then Thursday at 10:30, and, you know. Friday at one in the afternoon, and their schedule's all messed up, they just follow a daily routine, okay? Ever since I was probably like 19 years old, I wake up at 5:30 in the morning, okay? Sometimes I wake up at five now. I'm congruent, I'm scheduled. On the weekends, I might sleep in till 6:30.
maybe 7 a. m. on Saturday, Sunday, just depends on if I got anything done. During the work week, I'm up early, going to the gym, I'm getting things done. I'm congruent, following a daily routine. Also, what congruency does is it builds self-confidence because you do what you say you're gonna do, okay? And if you say you're gonna do something and then things change and you can't do it, you tell the person or company or whatever, 'Hey, something came up and here's why I can't do this.' You don't just like go MIA. Okay, so you gotta be congruent. I will tell you, you start doing that, you're congruent, doing what you say, it builds trust with everyone you're around, okay?
